creator: pmc@citylink.dinoex.sub.org
text:
Created attachment 230941
cppunit.log

Compiling devel/cppunit with DOXYGEN=ON, these errors were received: 

pkg-static: Unable to access file /var/local/ports/usr/ports/devel/cppunit/work/stage/usr/local/share/doc/cppunit/html/dir_000001_000008.html:No such file or directory
pkg-static: Unable to access file /var/local/ports/usr/ports/devel/cppunit/work/stage/usr/local/share/doc/cppunit/html/dir_000003_000008.html:No such file or directory
pkg-static: Unable to access file /var/local/ports/usr/ports/devel/cppunit/work/stage/usr/local/share/doc/cppunit/html/dir_000008_000005.html:No such file or directory

(Full log is attached)

Further analysis shows that the port does compile to random filenames, dependent on which type of filesystem it is compiled on.

One time I get these files (that works):
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000001_000002.html 
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000001_000005.html 
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000001_000006.html 
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000001_000008.html 
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000003_000008.html 
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000008_000005.html 
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000010_000000.html 

the other time I get these files (that does not work):
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000001_000002.html                       
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000001_000005.html                       
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000001_000006.html                       
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000001_000007.html                       
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000003_000006.html                       
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000006_000005.html                       
work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc/html/dir_000010_000000.html                       


To reproduce the behaviour:

$ cd /usr/ports/devel/cppunit
$ make clean; make
$ cd work/cppunit-1.14.0/include
$ mv cppunit .cppunit ; mkdir cppunit

$ for i in `ls .cppunit`; do
> ( cd .cppunit ; cp -R $i ../cppunit )
> done

$ cd ../doc ; rm -r html
$ make
$ ls html/dir_0000*
html/dir_000001_000002.html     html/dir_000003_000006.html
html/dir_000001_000005.html     html/dir_000006_000005.html
html/dir_000001_000006.html     html/dir_000010_000000.html
html/dir_000001_000007.html

$ cd ../include
$ rm -r cppunit; mkdir cppunit
$ for i in `ls .cppunit | sort -r`; do
> ( cd .cppunit ; cp -R $i ../cppunit )
> done

$ cd ../doc ; rm -r html
$ make
$ ls html/dir_0000*
html/dir_000001_000002.html     html/dir_000003_000008.html
html/dir_000001_000005.html     html/dir_000008_000005.html
html/dir_000001_000006.html     html/dir_000010_000000.html
html/dir_000001_000008.html


The meaningful thing in this command sequence is the "sort -r": these output files obtain different names depdendent on the *raw directory order* of the source files (which is dependent on the filesystem type and other subtle conditions).

Anyway, what the port does at that place is:
$ cd /usr/ports/devel/cppunit/work/cppunit-1.14.0/doc; make -n
"/usr/local/bin/doxygen"

so this should probably be a defect at doxygen (on feature "DIRECTORY_GRAPH").
